step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the provided explanation but want to add an additional point regarding why S4 is particularly useful clinically. When auscultating for signs of ventricular stiffness, clinicians often look for the presence of a S4 because its occurrence can be correlated with systolic hypertension and left ventricular hypertrophy, common causes of ventricular stiffness. Additionally, since S4 typically appears later in life compared to younger individuals without these pathologies, it helps differentiate between age-related changes and pathological ones. These practical considerations further support using S4 as the primary diagnostic tool for identifying stiff ventricular walls.
